title: Method and system for providing active tissue site debridement
abstract: A wound debridement system includes a wound dressing having an active layer and a wound interface layer. The active layer is formed from one or more transmission layers that are arranged about a central post. Activation of the transmission layers is configured to cause the movement of the wound interface layer relative to a tissue site to which the wound dressing is applied. A drive unit is operably attached to the central post. The drive unit is configured to generate and transfer a vibrational energy and/or a rotational movement to the transmission layer(s) via the central post. The resultant vibration and/or rotation of the transmission layer(s) is imparted onto the wound interface layer, thereby effectuating the desired movement of the wound interface layer relative to the tissue site, which allows for the debridement of debris that may be located at the tissue site.
claims: Claims WE CLAIM: 1. An active debridement wound dressing comprising: a wound interface layer configured to contact a wound and mechanically debride the wound when the wound interface layer moves relative to the wound; and an active layer coupled to the wound interface layer and comprising a transmission layer extending radially outwards relative to a central post, the transmission layer being configured to vibrate when activated so as to move the wound interface layer relative to the wound and mechanically debride the wound. 2. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 1, wherein the transmission layer is formed from a plurality of arm members. 3. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 2, wherein the plurality of arm members form an interconnected, interrupted planar structure. 4. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 3, wherein the arm members are arranged such that the interconnected planar structure is defined by a spiderweb-like pattern. 5. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 3, wherein the arm members are arranged such that the interconnected planar structure is defined by one or more concentric circles. 6. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 3, wherein the arm members are arranged such that the interconnected planar structure is defined by a spoke-like pattern surrounded by a radially extending outer perimeter. 7. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 1, wherein the wound interface layer is formed from a foam- like material. 8. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 7, wherein a plurality of through- holes extend through the wound interface layer, the plurality of through-holes being distinct from pores which define the foam-like material from which the wound interface layer is formed. 9. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 7, further comprising an absorbent layer, the absorbent layer being distinct and separate from the wound interface layer. 10. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 9, wherein the transmission layer is positioned on top of a top surface of the wound interface layer and below a lower surface of the absorbent layer. 11. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 1, wherein the transmission layer is at least partially embedded within the wound interface layer. 12. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 11, wherein the transmission layer is entirely embedded within the wound interface layer. 13. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 2, wherein the arm members are formed from a semi-rigid material. 14. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 1, wherein the active layer comprises a plurality of vertically spaced transmission layers. 15. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 1, wherein the transmission layer is fixedly attached to the central post. 16. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 1, wherein the transmission layer is movably attached to the central post, such that the transmission layer may be rotated relative to the central post. 17. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 1, wherein the transmission layer is configured to vibrate in response to a vibrational energy being transmitted to the transmission layer by the central post. 18. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 17, further comprising a drive unit configured to generate a vibrational energy operably attached to the central post. 19. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 18, further comprising a control unit coupled to the drive unit, the control unit configured to activate the drive unit to generate a vibrational energy that is transmitted to the transmission layer. 20. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 1, further comprising a drape layer sealable to a patientâs skin surrounding the wound and configured to maintain the wound dressing at a desired treatment area. 21. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 20, further comprising a semi-rigid support layer positioned between the drape layer and the wound dressing, the support layer configured to prevent the wound dressing from sticking to the drape layer and to prevent the wound dressing from being unintentionally displaced from the desired treatment area in response to a vibrational energy being applied to the wound dressing. 22. An active debridement wound dressing comprising: a wound interface layer configured to contact a wound and mechanically debride the wound when the wound interface layer moves relative to the wound; and an active layer coupled to the wound interface layer and comprising a transmission layer extending radially outwards relative to a central post, the transmission layer being configured to rotate when activated so as to move the wound interface layer relative to the wound and mechanically debride the wound. 23. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, wherein the transmission layer is formed from a plurality of arm members. 24. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 23, wherein the plurality of arm members form an interconnected, interrupted planar structure. 25. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 24, wherein the arm members are arranged such that the interconnected planar structure is defined by a spiderweb-like pattern. 26. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 24, wherein the arm members are arranged such that the interconnected planar structure is defined by one or more concentric circles. 27. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 24, wherein the arm members are arranged such that the interconnected planar structure is defined by a spoke-like pattern surrounded by a radially extending outer perimeter. 28. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, wherein the wound interface layer is formed from a foam- like material. 29. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 28, wherein a plurality of through- holes extend through the wound interface layer, the plurality of through-holes being distinct from pores which define the foam-like material from which the wound interface is formed. 30. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 28, further comprising an absorbent layer. 31. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 30, wherein the transmission layer is positioned on top of a top surface of the wound interface layer and below a lower surface of the absorbent layer. 32. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, wherein the transmission layer is at least partially embedded within the wound interface layer. 33. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 32, wherein the transmission layer is entirely embedded within the wound interface layer. 34. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, wherein the arm members are formed from a semi-rigid material. 35. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, wherein the active layer comprises a plurality of vertically spaced transmission layers. 36. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, wherein the transmission layer is fixedly attached to the central post. 37. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, wherein the transmission layer is movably attached to the central post, such that the transmission layer may be rotated relative to the central post. 38. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, wherein the transmission layer is configured to rotate in response to a rotational movement being transmitted to the transmission layer by the central post. 39. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 38, further comprising a drive unit configured to generate a rotational movement operably attached to the central post. 40. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 39, further comprising a control unit coupled to the drive unit, the control unit configured to activate the drive unit to generate a rotational movement that is transmitted to the transmission layer via the central post. 41. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, further comprising a drape layer sealable to a patientâs skin surrounding the wound and configured to maintain the wound dressing at a desired treatment area. 42. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 41, further comprising a semi-rigid support layer positioned between the drape layer and the wound dressing, the support layer configured to prevent the wound dressing from sticking to the wound dressing and to prevent the wound dressing from being unintentionally displaced from the desired treatment area in response to a rotation movement being applied to the wound dressing. 43. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 40, wherein the rotational movement that is transmitted to the transmission layer is configured rotate the transmission layer relative to the central post. 44. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 43, the rotational movement that is transmitted to the central post being configured to rotate the central post relative to the wound. 45. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 43, wherein the central post is configured to remain stationary relative to the wound when the rotational movement is transmitted to the central post. 46. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 40, wherein the rotational movement that is transmitted to the central post is configured to rotate the central post relative to the wound. 47. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 46, wherein the transmission layer is configured to remain stationary relative to the central post in response to the transmitted rotational movement from the drive unit. 48. The active debridement wound dressing of Claim 22, wherein the transmission layer is configured to rotate in an orbital motion. 49. An active debridement wound therapy system comprising: a wound interface layer configured to contact a wound and mechanically debride the wound when the wound interface layer moves relative to the wound; an active layer coupled to the wound interface layer and comprising a transmission layer extending radially outwards relative to a central post, the transmission layer being configured vibrate when activated so as to move the wound interface layer relative to the wound and mechanically debride the wound; and a therapy unit separate from the wound dressing configured to generate and apply a vibrational energy to the active layer. 50. An active debridement wound therapy system comprising: a wound interface layer configured to contact a wound and mechanically debride the wound when the wound interface layer moves relative to the wound; an active layer coupled to the wound interface layer and comprising a transmission layer extending radially outwards relative to a central post, the transmission layer being configured to rotate when activated so as to move the wound interface layer relative to the wound and mechanically debride the wound; and a therapy unit separate from the wound dressing configured to apply a rotational movement to the active layer. 51. A method of debriding a wound comprising: providing a dressing including a wound interface layer configured to contact a wound and mechanically debride the wound when the wound interface layer moves relative to the wound; providing an active layer coupled to the wound interface layer, the active layer comprising a transmission layer configured to transmit vibration or oscillation across the wound dressing; and transmitting at least one of a vibrational or an oscillatory motion to the wound interface layer via the transmission layer. 52. The method of claim 51, wherein the transmission layer extends radially outwards relative to a central post. 53. The method of claim 51, further comprising providing a therapy unit separate from the wound dressing, the therapy unit configured to generate and apply at least one of a vibrational or an oscillatory motion to the active layer. 54. The method of Claim 51, wherein the transmission layer is positioned on top of a top surface of the wound interface layer. 55. The method of Claim 51, wherein the transmission layer is at least partially embedded within the wound interface layer. 56. The method of Claim 55, wherein the transmission layer is entirely embedded within the wound interface layer. 57. The method of Claim 52, further comprising rotating the transmission layer relative to the central post in response to the transmitted vibrational or oscillatory motion. 58. The method of Claim 51, further comprising positioning a semi-rigid support layer over the wound interface layer and the active layer, the support layer being configured to prevent the wound interface layer and the active layer from being unintentionally displaced from the desired treatment area. 59. The method of Claim 52, further comprising rotating the central post relative to the wound in response to the transmitted vibrational or oscillatory motion. 60. The method of Claim 52, further comprising maintaining the central post stationary relative to the wound while the vibrational or oscillatory motion is transmitted to the wound interface layer. 61. The method of Claim 52, further comprising maintaining the transmission layer stationary relative to the central post while the vibrational or oscillatory motion is transmitted to the wound interface layer. 62. The method of Claim 52, further comprising maintaining the central post stationary relative to the wound while the vibrational or an oscillatory motion is transmitted to the wound interface layer. 63. The method of Claim 51, further comprising rotating the transmission layer in an orbital motion in response to the transmitted vibrational or oscillatory motion.
